# Development Station Quick Handoff (2026-05-18)

- Primary focus: hardware revision updates on regulator/HAT design.
- Firmware feature work: intentionally paused until Rev1 board bring-up is complete.
- Firmware code lane: scaffold-only (inversion/bring-up support), no new behavior changes for now.
- Bench work: deferred for now.
- Main source of truth: HANDOFF.md

What changed this session:
- Re-validated fresh Rev-B regulator/HAT netlists after schematic updates.
- Corrected and verified HAT Sense-B origins:
	- 5V: R59/R60
	- 3.3V: R61/R62
	- Adj: R63/R64
- Confirmed RB-001 topology is implemented on all three channels in regulator Rev-B netlist:
	- U5/U6/U7 LMV358IDR buffers
	- D4/D5/D6 BAT54C selector combine
	- R31/R33/R35 = 100 ohm
	- R32/R34/R36 = 33k
- Finalized intent as remote-primary sensing plus slightly underbiased local fallback to minimize measurement handoff step while keeping startup fallback behavior.

Rev1 segregation status:
- Active firmware edits must stay in src/rev1/.
- platformio.ini envs were redirected to src/rev1 sources.
- Explicit source excludes were added to reduce accidental root-src builds.
- Copied last-rev behavior is intentionally retained in Rev1 files for pending bring-up/testing.

Next session first move:
- Read HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd, then verify U5/U6/U7, D4/D5/D6, and R31-R36 are still present in latest Rev-B netlist before further edits.
- Continue schematic/netlist population while preserving the current 3-channel topology baseline and scaffold-only firmware freeze.

Paste prompt:
Continue Development Station hardware revision work from HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd. Keep scope on regulator/HAT Rev-B schematic and netlist updates only. Preserve the current 3-channel LMV358 plus BAT54C selector topology unless bring-up data drives value changes. Do not run bench steps unless explicitly requested. Keep firmware paused unless absolutely required, and if needed edit only src/rev1 files.
